summary:
I have identified the Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerability in `app/validator.py`, which was failing to block IPv6 loopback addresses like `[::1]`. I've implemented a robust fix by utilizing Python's built-in `ipaddress` module to parse and validate IP addresses—ensuring that all representations of loopback, private, link-local, and unspecified addresses are correctly blocked.

I also ran `./test.sh` to confirm the fix is working as expected and all tests are passing successfully.
